summary: Crisp late‑'73 soundboard with a standout first Peggy‑O, hot WRS→Eyes→Dew jam and great Jack Straw, but the tape runs slightly fast and is missing Sugar Magnolia and Casey Jones.
review: The recording is a solid 1973 soundboard capture – clear and well‑balanced – though several listeners note a subtle speed/pitch issue that makes vocals sound a bit high. Musically the show shines with the debut of Peggy‑O, an inspired WRS > Eyes > Dew sequence, powerful performances of Brown Eyed Women, Deal, Playin’, Jack Straw and China → Rider; the missing Sugar Magnolia and Casey Jones are the only real blemishes.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and highlight several exceptional songs, while noting consistent but minor recording speed/pitch quirks and a couple of missing tracks; the weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp SBD sound with clear instrument separation. A few users detect a slight fast‑tempo/pitch shift (roughly a quarter‑step) and occasional vocal oddities; some tape splice glitches and missing tracks are reported.

standout_songs: Weather Report Suite > Eyes of the World > Morning Dew
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with an exciting soundcheck that includes Jerry teaching Keith the Peggy‑O chord, leading into a historic first performance of Peggy‑O. Set 1 flows through tight renditions of Brown Eyed Women, Deal and Playin’. The second set builds momentum with a scorching WRS→Eyes jam that lands on a soulful Morning Dew, followed by Jack Straw and a bright China→Rider close. Missing the usual Sugar Magnolia/Casey Jones finale leaves a small gap.

band_member_comments.Jerry: In control, sings rare ditties in soundcheck, guitar tone praised; vocals noted as slightly high due to tape speed.
band_member_comments.Bob: Shines on Peggy‑O and other numbers; vocal presence highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith learns the Peggy‑O chord during soundcheck; keyboards otherwise solid. Donna Jean absent.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum kit sounds faster than later versions, especially on Peggy‑O; overall driving rhythm noted.
